item.title: Messi gives Argentina 1-0 halftime lead over Algeria
item.description: Lionel Messi's first-half strike has given Argentina a 1-0 lead over Algeria at halftime in their opening FIFA World Cup Group J clash. The Argentine captain opened the scoring in the 17th minute, calmly finding the back of the net to put the South Americans ahead.
item.content:encoded:
Lionel Messi's first-half strike has given Argentina a 1-0 lead over Algeria at halftime in their opening FIFA World Cup Group J clash.

The Argentine captain opened the scoring in the 17th minute, calmly finding the back of the net to put the South Americans ahead.

Argentina dominated much of the first half with Messi pulling the strings in midfield and creating several attacking opportunities as Algeria struggled to break through a disciplined Argentine defence.

Algeria attempted to respond with a number of attacking moves but were unable to find an equaliser before the break.

The Group J encounter remains finely poised heading into the second half, with Argentina looking to secure all three points while Algeria searches for a way back into the contest.

Meanwhile, France earlier defeated Senegal 3-1, while Norway beat Iraq 4-1.

Stay with us for updates on the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Fijian Ferrari Jiuta Wainiqolo blazes to Top 14 Golden Boot with 18 tries
item.description: FIJI Water Flying Fijian winger and current Lyon star, Jiuta Wainiqolo commonly known as the Fijian Ferrari, ended his 2025/26 Top 14 season in France in outstanding fashion, finishing as the competition’s leading try scorer with 18 tries. The former Ratu Kadavulevu School student, who helped his school win the Dean’s Trophy in 2015, scored one try in every game for the first 7 games.
item.content:encoded:
FIJI Water Flying Fijian winger and current Lyon star, Jiuta Wainiqolo commonly known as the Fijian Ferrari, ended his 2025/26 Top 14 season in France in outstanding fashion, finishing as the competition’s leading try scorer with 18 tries.

The former Ratu Kadavulevu School student, who helped his school win the Dean’s Trophy in 2015, scored one try in every game for the first 7 games.

He showcased his blistering pace, attacking flair, and finishing ability to become one of the league’s most dangerous players.

Wainiqolo went on to score 18 tries in 18 regular-season appearances during the 2025/26 French Top 14 campaign, averaging an impressive one try per match.

He also etched his name into the record books by setting a new Top 14 record for the fastest hat-trick, scoring three tries in just 7 minutes and 23 seconds against Montauban.

item.title: Haaland scores twice as Norway beat Iraq 4-1
item.description: Star striker Erling Haaland scored twice in his FIFA World Cup debut as Norway beat Iraq 4-1 lead in their Group I clash.Haaland o

item.title: Mbappe scores twice as France beat Senegal 3-1 in World Cup
item.description: France secured a 3-1 win over Senegal in their FIFA World Cup Group I match, with star striker Kylian Mbappé scoring twice in the second half. After a scoreless first half, Mbappe opened the scoring in the 66th minute following an assist from Michael Olise .
item.content:encoded:
France secured a 3-1 win over Senegal in their FIFA World Cup Group I match, with star striker Kylian Mbappé scoring twice in the second half.

After a scoreless first half, Mbappe opened the scoring in the 66th minute following an assist from Michael Olise .

Mbappe is also now the all-time leading goalscorer for Les Bleus.

France doubled their lead in the 82nd minute through Bradley Barcola , with Adrien Rabiot providing the assist.

Senegal reduced the deficit in stoppage time when Ibrahima Mbaye scored in the 95th minute.

However, Mbappe struck again a minute later to complete his brace and seal the victory for France in the Group I encounter.

Meanwhile in other matches, Iraq will face Norway at 10am, Argentina will meet Algeria at 1pm, while Austria will face Jordan at 4pm. Stay with us for updates on the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Flying Fijians among world's best as Nations Championship Trophy unveiled
item.description: Fiji's place among the world's rugby elite has been reinforced with the unveiling of the inaugural Nations Championship Trophy, as

item.title: Blues on red alert for Reece Walsh's roving role
Norway take 2-1 lead over Iraq into halftime
item.description: Norway holds a 2-1 lead over Iraq at halftime in their FIFA World Cup Group I clash, with star striker Erling Haaland scoring both goals for the Norwegians. Haaland opened the scoring in the 29th minute to register his first-ever FIFA World Cup goal.
item.content:encoded:
Norway holds a 2-1 lead over Iraq at halftime in their FIFA World Cup Group I clash, with star striker Erling Haaland scoring both goals for the Norwegians.

Haaland opened the scoring in the 29th minute to register his first-ever FIFA World Cup goal.

Iraq fought back through Aymen Hussein, who equalised in the 39th minute after being set up by Amir Al Ammari.

However, Haaland struck again four minutes later to restore Norway's lead and give his side the advantage heading into the halftime break.

Group I also features France and Senegal, with France earlier defeating Senegal 3-1.

Argentina takes on Algeria at 1pm and Austria will face Jordan at 4pm.

Stay with us for more FIFA World Cup updates.

item.title: France and Senegal locked at nil-all in tense World Cup clash
item.description: France and Senegal remain deadlocked at nil-all in their FIFA World Cup Group I opener in New Jersey, with both sides creating chances but unable to find a breakthrough in a tense contest. Third-ranked France has seen more of the ball and has looked threatening through captain Kylian Mbappe and Ousmane Dembele, but Senegal's defence has held firm to keep the score level.
item.content:encoded:
France and Senegal remain deadlocked at nil-all in their FIFA World Cup Group I opener in New Jersey, with both sides creating chances but unable to find a breakthrough in a tense contest.

Third-ranked France has seen more of the ball and has looked threatening through captain Kylian Mbappe and Ousmane Dembele, but Senegal's defence has held firm to keep the score level.

Senegal has also posed a threat going forward, using its pace and counterattacking ability to test the French backline as it looks to repeat its famous upset victory over France at the 2002 FIFA World Cup.

The match has lived up to expectations so far, with both teams showing attacking intent in their bid to secure an important result in the opening Group I fixture.

France entered the tournament as one of the favourites to win the World Cup, while Senegal arrived determined to prove it can compete with the world's top football nations.

The winner of the clash will take an early advantage in Group I, which also includes Iraq and Norway.

Meanwhile in other matches, Iraq will face Norway at 10am, Argentina will meet Algeria at 1pm while Austria will face Jordan at 4pm.

item.description: Third-ranked France will take on 15th-ranked Senegal in a highly anticipated FIFA World Cup Group I clash in less than two hours,

item.title: Elijah Just's double earns New Zealand a point against Iran
item.description: Elijah Just scored twice as New Zealand fought back to secure a 2-2 draw against Iran in their FIFA World Cup Group G clash.Just w

item.title: Uruguay fight back to earn 1-all draw against Saudi Arabia
item.description: Uruguay fought back from a goal down to earn a 1-1 draw against Saudi Arabia in their FIFA World Cup Group H opener in Miami.Urugu
